---
title: "OCR Alignment"
---

**[`aligner.py`](aligner.py)** aligns OCR'd text to a reference (ground-truth) text using
confidence-weighted sequential search, based on the algorithm described in
[Chiron et al. (2017)](https://link.springer.com/chapter/10.1007/978-3-030-22871-2_58).

Input can be plain text or hOCR (Tesseract output). Alignment uses exact and
fuzzy (Levenshtein) forward/backward window search, with confidence scores
adjusted by dictionary membership.

```bash
# Plain text input, TSV output
python aligner.py ocr.txt reference.txt

# hOCR input with bounding boxes, JSON output
python aligner.py --hocr --format json ocr.hocr reference.txt
```

**Output fields:** `ocr_word`, `ref_word`, `ref_position`, `confidence`, `failed`;
with hOCR input also `bbox`, `x_wconf`, `word_id`, `page_num`.

**Dependencies:** `rapidfuzz`; optionally `beautifulsoup4` + `lxml` (hOCR), `pyenchant` (dictionary check).
